RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 101-E, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Marlene “Yvonne” Terry, a Democrat, serves St. Louis County (District 66) in the Missouri House of Representatives. Her district includes Spanish Lake, Bellefontaine Neighbors, Glasgow Village, the City of Riverview, and Castle Point. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2020.
Before serving in the legislature, Terry worked as an accounting clerk for BJC Healthcare for 20 years. Marlene Terry was the first African American elected to Riverview Gardens School Board. During her nine years of service, Terry held various leadership roles on the board, including secretary and vice president, and later served as the President of the School Board.
Marlene Terry is the former chairwoman of the Missouri Legislative Black Caucus and the Founder of the Black Caucus Foundation of Missouri, established in 2024. She serves on the Legislative Review Committee, Corrections Committee, Ethics Committee, and the Committee for Children and Families.
Born in St. Louis, Terry attended University City High School and UMSL. She attends Olivet Missionary Baptist Church in Florissant. She has two children and five grandchildren.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to the custody and support of children
Provides that a grandparent shall be given priority if it is in the best interest of the child to be placed in the custody of a third party
Establishes the "Study Commission on Grandparents Raising Grandchildren"
Modifies penalties relating to criminal offenses involving the custody of children
Modifies provisions relating to guardianships and conservatorships
Provides that the court shall not order a parent to pay child support to the other parent for reasonable or necessary expenses of the child if the parents are awarded equal time with the child
Requires utilities to reimburse customer losses due to power outages
Requires the state board of education to provide for a transition to a special administrative board for certain unaccredited school districts
Establishes the "Motivational Boot Camp Incarceration Program" in the department of corrections and allows certain persons to be sentenced to the program
Changes the laws regarding cities of the fourth classification

Modifies provisions related to the "circuit breaker" property tax credit, reenacts the "Missouri Homestead Preservation Tax Credit Program", and implements a homestead exemption for certain individuals
Prohibits a political subdivision from adopting policies for property transferred by political subdivisions from being used for lawful education purposes by a charter school
Establishes provisions governing recalls of certain city and village officials
Authorizes a tax credit for certain volunteer drivers
Requires school districts to provide instruction in cursive writing
Proposes a constitutional amendment that reduces property tax assessments on senior citizens and disabled persons by fifty percent

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Prohibits certain discriminatory practices on the basis of race
Modifies provisions relating to good time credit
Establishes provisions relating to freestanding claims of actual innocence
Modifies provisions relating to the licensing of persons performing funeral-related services
Establishes certain protections for vulnerable persons
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
